Terms.title: Terms & Guidelines for Organizers
Terms.subtitle: Content, operational, and payout rules when creating and managing events on EvoTicket
Terms.warning_desc: Certain violations may lead to rejection, removal of the event, or suspension of payout. Organizers should review content, ticket config, and payout info before submitting.

a.title: A. Overview
a.subtitle: Tool scope and usage responsibilities
a.desc: EvoTicket provides Organizers with tools to create events, sell tickets, report revenue, manage resale, and coordinate checkers at the gate. Organizers may only manage events they own or are authorized to within their organization.
a.r1_desc: Each Organizer account can only act on events within its workspace. Accessing events outside of this scope violates the terms of use.

b.title: B. Content & Image Rules
b.subtitle: Standardizing public content on EvoTicket
b.r1_desc: Banners, posters, and descriptions must not contain phone numbers, emails, or external links intended to drive off-platform transactions.
b.r2_desc: Images must comply with community standards, respect copyrights, and avoid impersonating other brands.
b.r3_desc: Cover and poster images must fit EvoTicket's display ratio, ensuring important details aren't cropped out.
b.r4_desc: Ticket descriptions must accurately reflect actual benefits. Avoid vague descriptions that mislead regarding ticket class, zone, or accompanying experiences.

c.title: C. Event Creation Rules
c.subtitle: Required fields and validity conditions
c.r1_desc: Name, description, event type, format (Offline/Online/Hybrid), and schedule must be fully declared before submission.
c.r2_title: Valid time, location, and ticket types
c.r2_desc: Start time cannot be in the past. Ticket types must have clear prices, quantities, and sales windows.
c.r3_desc: When using a seat map, each seat must be assigned to a corresponding zone and ticket type. Blocked seats must be clearly marked.
c.r4_title: System may reject if missing configs
c.r4_desc: Events missing payout info, valid ticket types, or violating content rules will not be published.

d.title: D. Tickets & Resale
d.subtitle: Ticket configuration and secondary market constraints
d.r1_title: Organizer configures ticket types and seat map
d.r1_desc: Organizers configure ticket types, quantities, benefits, and assign seats if needed. Changes after sales start may be limited.
d.r2_title: Resale is bound by platform policies
d.r2_desc: EvoTicket applies general resale policies. Some ticket classes may be banned from resale or have transfer limits.
d.r3_title: Max resale price and royalty fee
d.r3_desc: The maximum resale price and royalty rate are key configurations affecting secondary revenue. Must be set before sales begin.
d.r4_desc: Tickets in 'locked' status cannot be traded on the secondary market. This is usually due to ongoing transfers, verification, or operational locks.

e.title: E. Check-in & Checkers
e.subtitle: Gate responsibilities and offline mode
e.r1_desc: Organizers are responsible for assigning, revoking, and supervising checkers. The checker list is audited in reconciliation reports.
e.r2_title: Checkers act only on assigned events/gates
e.r2_desc: Checkers cannot check-in attendees for unassigned events or gates.
e.r3_desc: The checker app works offline and syncs when back online. Conflicts are resolved according to platform rules.

f.title: F. Payout & Reconciliation
f.subtitle: Payout process and data requirements
f.r1_desc: Organizers must declare the correct account holder, bank, and account number for payouts.
f.r2_desc: Mismatches between legal entity and payout account may result in held payouts pending verification.
f.r3_desc: Organizers can export revenue, check-in, resale, and buyer list reports for basic auditing.

g.title: G. Organizer Responsibilities
g.subtitle: Commitments when using the platform
g.r1_desc: Organizers must ensure the event takes place at the stated time, location, and with the content promised to ticket buyers.
g.r2_desc: All complaints regarding the event experience are the Organizer's responsibility, within the scope allowed by platform policies.
g.r3_desc: Buyer data may only be used for event operations. Do not share, sell, or use it for out-of-scope purposes.

h.title: H. Violations & Handling
h.subtitle: Handling levels depending on violation severity
h.r1_desc: Events failing to meet content or config requirements will be returned to draft status with a detailed reason.
h.r2_desc: Events found violating policies after publishing may have sales locked or be removed from public view.
h.r3_desc: Suspicious transactions or major complaints may result in temporarily held payouts until review is complete.
h.r4_desc: Resale fraud, checker fraud, or misleading content will be handled per platform policy, up to suspending the organizer account.

i.title: I. Support
i.subtitle: Contact when policy clarification is needed

Terms.ack: I have read and understood the organizer guidelines
